Thou fool! love get, and sin cast from thy mind! That day when none is nigh thy God thou 'It find. 2. Sab chatrāī khāk milāī, tere hāth bikānī, Dekh, divāne, bhaī bāvrī, chit nahīṅ rahā ṭhikānī . Jhūlnā. My wit and skill mixed with the dust, to you myself I've sold, Dost see, O fool! I'm crazy grown—distracted me behold! 3. Sāīn, apne chitt ki bhūl na kahiye koë, Tab lag man meṅ rākhye, jab lag kāraj hoë . Girdhar Kuṅḍalyā. My lord, your error to another ne'er disclose, While in your mind it lies effect what you propose. 4. Sajjan chit kabhū nā dharat durjan jan ke bol, Pāhan māre ām ko tau jhal det amol . def: s.m. Bark, rind; skin, hide; the uneatable part of a fruit; half-eaten fruit; the fruit of the fan-palm; the cocoanut; the plantain.
s.m. An empty-pated fellow, a blockhead, a simpleton; — a cow that is nearly dry.
more: n. m. 1. A fool; simpleton. 2. A cow who gives but little milk.

def: s.m. Endearing arts and expressions, playfulness; toying, toyishness, fondling, dalliance, blandishment, coquetry; — pleasantry, humour; amusement, sport; — airs, affectation.
more: n. m. H. choṅch beak. 1. Fondling; billing and cooing; dalliance; blandishments; endearing arts and expressions; coquetry. 2. Pleasantry; jeu d'esprit ; humour. 3. Airs; affectation. Hosh kī apne kuchh davā kīje, Mujh se nā-haq na chochlā kīje . Wom. Shauq. Go, get some medicine for your want of sense, Your liberties to me are an offence. 4. Sport; luxury. Amīroṅ kā chochlā . The sports of great men. buḍḍhā chochlā . Blandishments in old age.
s. m. Playfulness, blandishments, endearing arts and expressions, coquetry, toyishness.

def: (rt. of ćod-nā+ghar), s.m. bawdy house.

ety: ćod˚ = S. चोदय(ति), caus. fr. rt. चुद
def: v.t. To copulate with, to have sexual intercourse with.
more: v. a. S. चुद to drive in. To copulate; to have sexual intercourse with. Chudāve chūt-marānī, chode beṭī-chod . Slang. Prov.
(tr. of چدنا) v. a. To copulate.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑁄𑀤𑁆𑀤𑀢𑀺 (*coddati, “to copulate”), from Sanskrit चोदति (codati, “to incite”).

def: adj. & s.m. Addicted to venery, lustful, lascivious, libidinous ( = ćudāsā, ćudakkaṛ)
more: adj. Lascivious; libidinous; lustful; in heat; excited. mūṅh-chodū , n. m. Slang . A boaster; blusterer; pseudo-gallant. chod'ā , Slang. n. m. An abusive term for a son. Mere bāp kā chodā nāhīṅ māne . Rus. Wom. This b— gg—d by his father's spawn of my womb wont mind me. chodī , Slang. n. f. A mistress; concubine.
subs: a lascivious man; — an improvident fellow

subs: a lascivious man; — an improvident fellow

def: s.m. A kind of cloak like a morning-gown (made of wool or camel's hair).
s.m. A kind of cloak like a morning-gown (made of wool or camel's hair).
more: n. m. A Mogul overcoat; a cloak; toga.

def: s.m. Food of birds; food or birds brought up from the crop
more: n. m. H. chugna to pick up. 1. Food of birds brought up from the crop. 2. Food of birds in general. Chiṛyā kā chogā khātā hai. chogā badalnā, dānā badlī k. , v. n. To caress, as doves by contact of their bills and feeding each other; to bill and coo.
s. m. 1. Food of birds brought up from the crop. 2. Food of birds in general. چوگا بدلنا chogā badalnā, To bill, to caress as doves by joining bills.
subs: To feed its young (the parent bird); to bill, to caress as doves, &c. by joining bills

def: s.m. A small well (cf. ćoyā).

ety: prob. S. उत्सव+ल+इका; cf. also ćaććar, and S. चर्चरी
def: adj. Merry, mirthful, gay; amusing, pleasant
subs: ćahal-pahal, s.f. = ćǒhal; — (also) life, stir, bustle, activity
v.t. To carol, to be gay or cheerful, to make merry, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Comic, jocose; merry, gay; amusing, pleasant; — a jester, wag ( = ćǒhlī).

ety: ćuhal or ćǒhal, q.v.+Prk. इओ = S. इकः(इन्+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. Comic, jocose; merry, gay; amusing, pleasant; — a jester, a wag, a merry fellow.
s.m. Name of a mountain-tribe ( = ćoʼāṛ, q.v.). s.m. Name of a mountain tribe who are robbers; a mountain-robber, a mountaineer and outlaw ( = ćohāṛ).
s.m. The jaw
s.m. A large wooden peg or pin, a tent-peg; a prop.

s.m. A wonder, marvel; — subtleness, delicacy, nicety, minuteness, fineness; grace, beauty; point, wit; excellence, merit: trick, prank; subtlety, artfulness, trick, fraud; — ardent desire, ardour, eagerness, longing, fondness (cf. ćān-o-ćoz).
adj. Subtle, fine, nice, delicate, &c.
adj. (prov.) = ćokhā, q.v.
subs: s.m. Seed sown immediately after a fall of rain.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀉𑀓𑁆𑀓 (caükka), from Sanskrit चतुष्क (catuṣka).

def: s.m. Husk or refuse of wheat, barley, &c.; bran.
more: Sant. n. m. The husk of wheat, barley, etc.; bran.
